entry.title: Xpath and CSS selector
entry.content:
Xpath Selector


ExpressionDescription
nodenameselect nodes with the name
.select the current node
..select the parant of current node
/select from root node
//select from this node no matter where they are
@select attributes
*match any elem node
@*match any attribute node


Node predicateDescription
[num]-
[last()]-
[position()]-
[@id=””]-
[@class=””]-



CSS Selector


SelectorExampleDescription
.class.navselect elems whose class is “nav”
#id#fruitselect elems whose id is “fruit”
**select all elems
elempselect <p> elems
elem, elemdiv, pselect <div> elems and <p> elems
elem elemdiv pselect <p> elems inside <div> elems
elem>elemdiv > pselect <p> elems whose parent is <div>
[attribute][target]select elems with a target attrtbute
[attr~=v][title~=flower]whose title contains flower
[attr^=v]a[href^=”https”]whose href begins with “https”
[attr$=v]a[href$=”com”]whose href ends with “com”
[attr*=v]a[href*=”engine”]whose href contains “engine”

entry.title: How to build a simple django project
entry.content:
0. Preface

install python 3.6
install pycharm professional 2018.2
install django 2.1



1. Create the project

cmd input django-admin startproject <project_name> to start a project

if your project name is my_blog, just input django-admin startproject my_blog



2. Run the project to test

cmd input python manage.py runserver to run it




3. Create the first app:

cmd inputdjango-admin startapp <app_name> to start an applications

if your app name is blog, just input django-admin startproject blog

don not forget to add it to settings.py!!!!!!!!!
(this simple mistake cost me 3 hours to find a css mistake solution)




4. Create the first page:

blog->views.py:(to write the page)


my_blog->urls.py:(this method just a simple test, which is deprecated)

(the url pattern include three parameters:URL(RegEx),method and name)

execute the second step to runserver, don’t forget to add /index to the end of the url to see your app page:Hello world



5. Enhance the url configuration

the method in step 4 is clumsy when facing a crowd of urls, this method will handle this problem:

my_blog->urls.py:


new a urls.py python file in blog folder, and write these(like the step 4):

then open http://127.0.0.1:8000/index/ you will see the page.

(if you want to make another page from the app, such as http://127.0.0.1:8000/index/article_1, remember the Regex should be re_path(r'article_1/$'), don’t forget the last /)

then the url-logic will be more explicit



6.Write a template

Django has its own template language: Django-Template-Language(DTL)

we can also choose other language by changing the settings.py


new a folder named ‘template’ in blog folder

new a HTML file in the template folder, for example: index.html(write something)

then change the settings.py file to fill the path of templates:

you should change to the render method to get the page:
blog->views.py

render has three parameters, the third is a dict parameter,which is for transmission(from backend to the template)

you can use double braces in template to use this parameter

e.g.
blog->views.py

index.html

if you want to link the html file with css, just refer to the external css file, or use statics like this:

new a folder named statics in blog(just the same level with templates folder), new css and pictures folder in it.

add these in html file to link to the specific css:

another question is coming, when we have a plenty of apps, the templates html will be confused by django. so in each app_templates folder we can new folder named that app name, and place html in it.

[exercise] try to create another app article



7.Models and Database

ORM(Object relation mapping)

Django has encapsulate the database operation

definite a class in the models of an app:

blog->models.py

generate a data sheet:

cmd input:
(preparation) $python manage.py makemigrations (app_name)
(without app_name, it means each apps will make a migration)

(migrate) $python manage.py migrate

now a database whose suffix is .sqlite3 will be create in the project root directory, you can manage the database.



8.Data transmission

back end blog->views.py

(if use all(), articles will be a list)


front end blog->templates->index.html

e.g.




9.Admin

cmd input : $python manage.py createsuperuser
(create a superadmin)

if you want a Chinese page, revamp the setting.py : LANGUAGE_CODE = 'zh_Hans'

let the admin page receive the database we created before:

article->admin

in the admin page, each article use Artical object as their name, it’s quite inconvenient:

article->models.py




10.New page development

Firstly create a new html file in templates folder, just abreast of index.html

When we want to click a hyperlink to go to the article_detail page, it’s essential to add a method in blog->views.py:


configure the url blog->urls.py

e.g. we can visit the first article just through localhost:8000/blog/article/1



11.Configurate the hyperlink

we want to realize a function that when we click the title of a specific article in the index page, the url will skip to the detial page of this article:

my_blog->urls.py


blog->urls.py


modify the index html page:
blog->templates->index.html




12.create the edit page

create a new html file:
blog->templates->blog->edit_page.html


comfigure a url for edit_page:
addre_path(r'^edit/$', views.edit_page, name='edit_page') to urlpatterns

comfigure a url for edit_action:
addre_path(r'^edit/$', views.edit_action, name='edit_action') to urlpatterns

add an action method in blog->views.py

add a hyperlink in ‘index.html’ to create a new article:

The above is how to create a new article, now we need to realize how to modify an existed article:


change the urlpatterns:


add a parameter (article_id) to the edit_page method:

then add an if judgement to the edit_page.html, by the way don’t forget to write another action page
(when create a new article, we make article_id 0)

improve edit_action method



All the steps above is a method to set up a primary django project. Thanks for reading.

entry.title: The basic operation of numpy
entry.content:
What is numpy?  Here is the definition of numpy library in wikipedia:

NumPy/ˈnʌmpaɪ/ is a library for Python, adding support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays.

In a word, numpy can make it more convenient for us to operate array and matrix in Python, with high efficiency.


Begin to use

Firstly, if you haven’t install this library please use pip install numpy to get it.

Typing numpy for many times is a waste, so we usually import numpy as np


Numpy Array & Matrix

propeties

  • ndim
  • shape
  • size

you can use slide operation to visit elements in an array, such as arr[:2,:4,2]

functions

[annotation]: if one of the params is determined, another can be set as -1 (means auto distribute)

combination
devide

Built-in mathematical operations

Arithmatics are aimming at each elements in the array/matrix

Aggregation operation

Index:
sort

you can add arg tag before the method’s name (e.g. argpartition, argsort), in this way it will return the array/matrix consist of indexs nums

It alse support axis param

fancy indexing:

using slice to select regular eles is enough, using fancy

logic operations between arrays or matrices will return its bool type

matplotlib (viusualization tool)

import matplotlib as mpl
always use its pyplot：import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

scatter diagram:

entry.title: Some issues and their solutions about coursera
entry.content:
Coursera is one of the most famous online learning platforms. However, some frustrating things could occur either, here are some issues I met and the solutions of them.

Problem1: the video is loading all the time:

method:

open your hosts file, for most windows users, it’s in C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc

add these lines to the bottom of your hosts file and save it

run cmd.exe, input ipconfig/flushdns then press Enter

refresh your coursera page, the video will play out successfully.


Problem2: the subtitles are too big/small

method:

inspect the page(by right click to choose or press F12), add video::-webkit-media-text-track-display {font-size: xx%;} to head-><style class="vjs-styles-defaults>" just like this:


(you can change the percentage value to find your perfect subtitle size)

to be continued…

entry.title: Use Atom as an elegant Markdown editor
entry.content:
Atom, I believe most of you may know about this editor. It is a free and open-source text editor developed by GitHub.

Atom is not only a source code editor, but an elegant Markdown editor. Here is the interface of my Atom when I use it as a Markdown editor.

In this passage, I will show you how I change it to an excellent Markdown editor by using some useful plug-ins in Windows 10.


Markdown Preview Enhanced

markdown-preview-enhanced is an extension that provide some useful functions, including

entry.title: Welcome to my Github Page
entry.content:
This personal website was built in Sep 19th, 2018, aiming to store and share my notes.

At first, I planed to set up a personal website by using Django frame + jQuery + MySQL + Apache, however, I find it so tough for me to design style and database. I can’t spend so much time to learn these knowledge of front end, and the cost for a damain name and web server is too high for a student. That’s the reason why I give up this idea.(But in the future I will build a dynamic page)

Then I thought of building my blog on Github Page, it’s free and easy to set up. Hexo is chosed to be the static website’s generator rather than Jekell or Hugo. After reading some relative documents, this website was set up as my personal blog page.

Whoever you are, thanks for visiting my blog. I hope some of my articles can be useful to you.
